With SKIP_VAULT=true, verify_cloud_db does not mark vault missing as failure. - With SKIP_CLOUD=true, verify_cloud_db does not mark cloud CLIs missing as failure.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T14:45:03.242319-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T14:52:41.60688-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T14:52:41.60688-05:00","close_reason":"Updated verify_cloud_db to honor SKIP_POSTGRES/SKIP_VAULT/SKIP_CLOUD so skips don't count as failures."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-6pto","title":"Task: Add credit card and email verification warnings to rent-vps","description":"","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:46:08.892768-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T13:14:44.855461-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T13:14:44.855461-05:00","close_reason":"Added credit card and email verification warnings to rent-vps page","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-6pto","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-qxj8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:52:38.507419-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-6r9o","title":"Enhance terminal copy/paste instructions with dedicated section","description":"# Task: Enhance Terminal Copy/Paste Instructions ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Terminal Onboarding Foundation (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ux1f) ## Current State Copy/paste instructions currently exist in: - generate-ssh-key/page.tsx Step 3 (Mac and Windows paste shortcuts) - ssh-connect/page.tsx Step 3 (same) However, users encounter copy/paste FIRST in install-terminal when downloading, so a dedicated section there would help. ## Enhancement Add a dedicated GuideSection in install-terminal/page.tsx SimplerGuide with comprehensive copy/paste instructions: ```tsx

Terminal copy/paste works a bit differently than other apps:

Mac (Ghostty/WezTerm)

  • ⌘ + C to copy
  • ⌘ + V to paste
  • Works just like other Mac apps!

Windows (Windows Terminal)

  • Right-click to paste (easiest!)
  • Or use Ctrl + Shift + C/V
  • Note: Ctrl + C STOPS commands, it does not copy!
 ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Consolidates copy/paste instructions in install-terminal (first encounter) - [ ] Mac instructions are clear and simple - [ ] Windows instructions warn about Ctrl+C danger - [ ] Uses kbd styling for key combinations - [ ] OS-conditional display (show only relevant OS) ## Note This enhances existing scattered mentions with a dedicated, comprehensive section.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:39:41.923436-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:21:11.767553-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:21:11.767553-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented: TerminalBasicsSection in install-terminal/page.tsx has dedicated copy/paste section with Mac (cmd+C/V) and Windows (right-click, Ctrl+Shift+V) instructions, including Ctrl+C warning for Windows"} -{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-6stg","title":"Task: Add SSH disconnect warning with reconnection instructions","description":"","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:47:15.391045-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T23:47:15.391045-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-6stg","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r1z5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:52:49.078227-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-6stg","title":"Task: Add SSH disconnect warning with reconnection instructions","description":"","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:47:15.391045-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T13:15:43.94899-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T13:15:43.94899-05:00","close_reason":"Addressed in r1z5 and existing run-installer page content: SSH disconnect warning (already present), installation output interpretation (added), curl command explanation (already present)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-6stg","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r1z5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:52:49.078227-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-6w9u","title":"Create dedicated /glossary page","description":"# Task: Create Dedicated Glossary Page ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Jargon System & Documentation Polish (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-7i45) ## Description Create a comprehensive, searchable glossary page where users can look up technical terms. This provides a deeper reference than tooltips and helps users who want to understand the concepts more fully. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/glossary/page.tsx \`\`\`tsx // Key features: // 1. Alphabetical listing of all terms // 2. Search/filter functionality // 3. Categorization (Shell, Networking, Tools, etc.) // 4. Detailed explanations (more than tooltip) // 5. Links to relevant wizard steps or lessons interface GlossaryEntry { term: string; short: string; // Tooltip version long: string; // Full explanation category: 'shell' | 'networking' | 'tools' | 'concepts'; relatedTerms?: string[]; learnMore?: string; // Link to lesson } \`\`\` Page should include: - Search box at top - Category filters - Clean, scannable layout - Mobile-friendly (single column) - Links from jargon tooltips to glossary entries ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All terms from Jargon component included - [ ] Search filters results in real-time - [ ] Categories help organize terms - [ ] Long explanations are thorough but accessible - [ ] Links to related wizard steps where applicable - [ ] Mobile-responsive design ## UI/UX Notes - This is a reference page, not a learning page - Users might land here from Google searches - Consider SEO implications (good meta descriptions) - Use same visual style as rest of site","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:45:10.252228-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:43:47.598957-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:43:47.598957-05:00","close_reason":"Completed (new /glossary page with live search + category filters; added Jargon tooltip links to glossary anchors)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-6w9u","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-367d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:57:43.702732-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-6y4w","title":"TASK: Implement prompt_ssh_key() function in user.sh","description":"## Parent Feature agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fslp (Installer SSH Key Integration) ## Summary Create the \`prompt_ssh_key()\` function that interactively prompts users to paste their SSH public key and installs it to /root/.ssh/authorized_keys. ## Implementation Details ### File Location \`scripts/lib/user.sh\` - add after existing functions ### Function Specification \`\`\`bash # Prompt user for SSH public key and install it # Called when running as root with no existing key # Returns 0 on success or skip, 1 on invalid key prompt_ssh_key() { # 1. Check if we already have a valid key if [[ -f /root/.ssh/authorized_keys ]]; then if grep -q "^ssh-" /root/.ssh/authorized_keys 2>/dev/null; then log_detail "SSH key already present, skipping prompt" return 0 fi fi # 2. Check if stdin is a terminal (interactive mode) if [[ ! -t 0 ]]; then log_warn "Non-interactive mode detected, skipping SSH key prompt" log_detail "You can add your key later with: ssh-copy-id" return 0 fi # 3. Display prompt UI echo "" echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗" echo "║ SSH Key Setup ║" echo "╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣" echo "║ Let's set up SSH key authentication so you won't need ║" echo "║ to enter a password every time you connect. ║" echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝" echo "" echo "Your public key should start with:" echo " ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1..." OR ssh-rsa AAAAB3NzaC1..." echo "" echo "You saved this earlier when you ran ssh-keygen on your computer." echo "(Press Enter to skip - you'll need password for future logins)" echo "" # 4. Read the key read -r -p "Paste your public key: " pubkey # 5. Handle skip if [[ -z "\$pubkey" ]]; then log_warn "SSH key setup skipped" log_detail "You can add your key later by running:" log_detail " echo 'your-key-here' >> ~/.ssh/authorized_keys" return 0 fi # 6. Validate key format if [[ ! "\$pubkey" =~ ^ssh-(ed25519|rsa|ecdsa|dss)[[:space:]] ]]; then log_error "Invalid SSH key format" log_detail "Expected format: ssh-ed25519 AAAA... or ssh-rsa AAAA..." log_detail "Make sure you copied the PUBLIC key (the .pub file)" return 1 fi # 7. Install the key mkdir -p /root/.ssh chmod 700 /root/.ssh echo "\$pubkey" >> /root/.ssh/authorized_keys chmod 600 /root/.ssh/authorized_keys log_success "SSH key installed successfully" log_detail "You can now connect with: ssh -i ~/.ssh/your_key root@this_ip" return 0 } \`\`\` ### Edge Cases to Handle 1. **Existing key**: Skip prompt if authorized_keys already has valid keys 2. **Non-interactive**: Skip if stdin is not a terminal (curl|bash mode) 3. **Empty input**: Treat as skip, show warning 4. **Invalid format**: Show helpful error, don't install 5. **Permission issues**: Create .ssh dir if missing, set proper perms ### Testing Scenarios 1. Fresh VPS, password-only → should prompt 2. VPS with existing key → should skip 3. Paste valid ed25519 key → should install 4. Paste valid rsa key → should install 5. Paste invalid text → should error 6. Press Enter (skip) → should warn and continue 7. Run via curl|bash → should skip gracefully ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Function exists in user.sh - [ ] Validates key format before installing - [ ] Handles all edge cases gracefully - [ ] Creates .ssh directory if missing - [ ] Sets correct permissions (700 for dir, 600 for file) - [ ] Works when run multiple times (idempotent) - [ ] Clear success/error messages ## Files Modified - scripts/lib/user.sh (add function)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:19:06.013332-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T19:31:59.551752-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T19:31:59.551752-05:00","close_reason":"Function already implemented in scripts/lib/user.sh lines 193-257. Handles existing key check, interactive mode detection, key validation, and proper permissions.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-6y4w","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fslp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:19:25.962058-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75e","title":"Core infrastructure for acfs-update","description":"## What Create the main acfs-update script skeleton with: - Argument parsing (categories, --yes, --dry-run, --verbose, --quiet, --continue/--abort-on-failure) - Logging infrastructure (to ~/.acfs/logs/updates/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS.log) - Progress display and summary generation - Helper functions for version detection, before/after capture - Checksum verification helpers (for stack updates) ## Why All category-specific update implementations depend on this foundation. Must be solid and well-tested before building on top. ## IMPORTANT: Execution Order Categories must run in a specific order at runtime (even if beads are implemented in parallel): 1. system - apt updates may affect Python, libraries 2. runtimes - bun, rust, uv depend on system packages 3. shell - may use runtimes 4. agents - use runtimes (bun for codex/gemini) 5. stack - use runtimes (uv for mcp_agent_mail, cargo for some tools) 6. cloud - use runtimes (bun for most CLIs) ## Script Structure - Main entry point with arg parsing - Category dispatch (run selected categories in order) - Per-tool update wrapper with logging - Summary generation at end ## Considerations - Script location: ~/.acfs/bin/acfs-update (added to PATH during install) - Logging must capture stdout/stderr from each update - Version detection varies by tool (--version, git describe, etc.) - For --dry-run: show what WOULD happen without doing it - For --yes: suppress all prompts, proceed automatically ## Success Criteria - [ ] acfs-update --help shows usage - [ ] --dry-run mode works (no actual changes) - [ ] Log file created with timestamps - [ ] Summary shows "X updated, Y skipped, Z failed" - [ ] Categories run in correct order","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:25:14.89257-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:08:16.127634-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:08:16.127634-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented core update infrastructure: logging, version detection, new CLI flags, abort-on-failure support","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75e","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-x0l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:25:20.750551-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} @@ -209,7 +209,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kyhv","title":"TASK: Add lesson completion celebration screen","description":"# TASK: Add lesson completion celebration screen ## Context When user completes a lesson, show a brief celebration before moving to next. ## Proposed Screen ``` ╭─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮ │ 🎉 Lesson Complete: tmux Basics │ │ │ │ You learned: │ │ • Creating sessions with ntm new │ │ • Attaching/detaching with Ctrl+B, D │ │ • Listing sessions with ntm list │ │ │ │ Progress: ████████████░░░░░░░░ 5/9 (56%) │ │ │ │ [Enter] Continue to next lesson │ │ [m] Return to menu │ ╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯ ``` ## Implementation ### Lesson Summary Data Add to each lesson markdown a summary section: ```markdown ``` Or define in onboard.sh as associative array. ### Celebration Function ```bash onboard_show_completion() { local lesson_num=$1 local lesson_title=$(get_lesson_title $lesson_num) local summary=$(get_lesson_summary $lesson_num) # Render celebration screen # Wait for keypress } ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Celebration shown after lesson completion - [ ] Shows lesson title and summary points - [ ] Shows updated progress bar - [ ] Enter continues, m returns to menu - [ ] Works with gum and plain fallback","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:59:49.050712-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T15:23:43.155042-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T15:23:43.155042-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented celebration screen with lesson summaries, progress bar, gum UI with fallback, and next/menu navigation","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kyhv","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-gjnn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T15:00:11.532879-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-l1rl","title":"Task: Create Troubleshooting page for common issues","description":"","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:52:01.286219-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T23:52:01.286219-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-l1rl","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-vsg0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:53:57.855209-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-l2w","title":"Harden sync_ntm_palette.sh: HTTPS-only curl + single-fetch hashing","description":"scripts/sync/sync_ntm_palette.sh currently uses plain curl -fsSL and may fetch the remote file twice when computing the hash. Align with scripts/lib/security.sh (HTTPS-only curl args + enforce_https) and compute remote hash from a single fetch.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenDog","created_at":"2025-12-20T20:46:02.630169-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T20:51:03.294991-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T20:51:03.294991-05:00","close_reason":"Resolved on main: sync_ntm_palette.sh now uses scripts/lib/security.sh for HTTPS-only curl + single-fetch hashing (commit d683f1f). Shellcheck clean.","labels":["scripts","security"]} -{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-l82e","title":"Task: Add installation output interpretation guide","description":"","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:47:20.770441-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T23:47:20.770441-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-l82e","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r1z5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:52:54.359928-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-lbo","title":"doctor: include user tool dirs in PATH","description":"scripts/lib/doctor.sh relies on PATH-only lookups, so running `acfs doctor` in a non-zshrc environment (fresh ssh, bash shell, etc.) can falsely report missing tools that are installed under ~/.local/bin, ~/.bun/bin, ~/.cargo/bin, etc. Make doctor self-contained by prepending common ACFS install dirs to PATH before checks (and optionally improve version detection for commands like go/tmux).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"FuchsiaCreek","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:46:21.82316-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T18:18:48.406739-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T18:18:48.406739-05:00","close_reason":"doctor self-contained PATH + version handling"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-lhe","title":"[ubu.10] Add user communication and progress tracking","description":"# Add User Communication and Progress Tracking ## Purpose Provide visibility to users during the multi-reboot upgrade process, especially when they SSH back in. ## Problem When a user SSH reconnects during/after an upgrade: 1. They have no idea what state the system is in 2. They might run install.sh again (could conflict) 3. They might try to use apt (could fail during upgrade) ## Solutions ### 1. MOTD (Message of the Day) Update Create temporary MOTD during upgrade: ```bash ubuntu_set_upgrade_motd() { cat > /etc/motd.d/99-acfs-upgrade </dev/null; then log_error "Another upgrade is in progress (PID: $pid)" return 1 fi # Stale lock file rm -f "$ACFS_UPGRADE_LOCK" fi echo $$ > "$ACFS_UPGRADE_LOCK" } ubuntu_release_lock() { rm -f "$ACFS_UPGRADE_LOCK" } ``` ### 3. Status Check Command Create simple status script: ```bash # /var/lib/acfs/check_upgrade_status.sh #!/usr/bin/env bash if [[ -f /var/lib/acfs/upgrade_state.json ]]; then echo "ACFS Ubuntu Upgrade Status" echo "==========================" cat /var/lib/acfs/upgrade_state.json | jq -r " \"Original: \" + .ubuntu_upgrade.original_version, \"Target: \" + .ubuntu_upgrade.target_version, \"Current: \" + ((.ubuntu_upgrade.completed_upgrades | length | tostring) + \"/\" + (.ubuntu_upgrade.upgrade_path | length | tostring)), \"Stage: \" + .ubuntu_upgrade.current_stage " else echo "No upgrade in progress" fi ``` ### 4. Pre-Reboot Warning Give users time to read before reboot: ```bash ubuntu_warn_reboot() { log_warn "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗" log_warn "║ System will reboot in 30 seconds for Ubuntu upgrade ║" log_warn "║ ║" log_warn "║ Your SSH session will disconnect. ║" log_warn "║ Wait 2-3 minutes, then reconnect. ║" log_warn "║ The upgrade will continue automatically. ║" log_warn "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝" for i in {30..1}; do echo -ne "\rRebooting in $i seconds... " sleep 1 done echo "" } ``` ### 5. Progress Estimation Show time estimates: ```bash ubuntu_show_progress() { local current_hop="$1" local total_hops="$2" local minutes_per_hop=45 local remaining_hops=$((total_hops - current_hop + 1)) local remaining_minutes=$((remaining_hops * minutes_per_hop)) log_info "Progress: Upgrade $current_hop of $total_hops" log_info "Estimated time remaining: ~${remaining_minutes} minutes" } ``` ## Integration Points 1. Set MOTD at start of upgrade phase 2. Clear MOTD when upgrade completes 3. Lock file acquired before upgrade, released after 4. Status script created during setup, removed during cleanup 5. Pre-reboot warning shown before each reboot","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:25:18.337553-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:40:19.26302-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T16:40:19.26302-05:00","close_reason":"Added lock file functions (upgrade_acquire_lock, upgrade_release_lock), progress display (upgrade_show_progress), reboot countdown warning (upgrade_warn_reboot), and status check script (upgrade_create_status_script). MOTD already implemented.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-lhe","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-9xt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:26:42.324328-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-lhe","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2yd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:26:42.526199-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-lwu7","title":"TASK: Implement acfs info terminal output renderer","description":"# TASK: Implement acfs info terminal output renderer ## Context Implement the terminal (default) output format for `acfs info`. Uses the data gathered by info.sh core functions. ## Implementation ### Function ```bash info_render_terminal() { # Use gum if available, fallback to plain output if command -v gum &>/dev/null; then info_render_terminal_gum else info_render_terminal_plain fi } ``` ### Gum-Enhanced Output Use gum style, gum format for rich terminal output with borders and colors. ### Plain Fallback ASCII-art borders, ANSI colors for minimal dependencies. ### Sections to Render 1. Header with ACFS branding 2. System section (hostname, IP, uptime, OS) 3. Installed Tools summary 4. Quick Commands (top 8) 5. Onboard Progress (visual bar) 6. Footer with next steps ### Color Scheme Match existing ACFS output (Catppuccin Mocha): - Cyan for headers - Green for success/installed - Yellow for skipped - Gray for secondary text ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Gum-enhanced output works - [ ] Plain fallback works without gum - [ ] Colors render correctly - [ ] Progress bar displays correctly - [ ] Output is readable on 80-column terminal","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:56:32.63759-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T15:07:45.632212-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T15:07:45.632212-05:00","close_reason":"Complete implementation: info.sh with terminal, JSON, HTML, minimal output formats; integrated into acfs CLI","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-lwu7","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3dkg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:56:49.986456-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} @@ -285,19 +285,19 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-qxj8","title":"Feature: Pre-Installation Clarity - Landing Page & Early Wizard","description":"","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:42:45.335788-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T13:11:48.986295-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T13:11:48.986295-05:00","close_reason":"Added Why VPS? section to landing page with 3 cards explaining laptop/cloud/24x7 benefits"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-qxr","title":"Generator: wrap upstream installer scripts with checksum verification","description":"acfs.manifest.yaml + scripts/generated/* currently embed unverified remote-script installs (curl|bash/sh). Align generator output with checksums.yaml + scripts/lib/security.sh so generated scripts execute upstream installers only after checksum verification, then regenerate outputs.","notes":"Generator now rewrites manifest curl|bash/sh installers to checksum-verified execution via scripts/lib/security.sh + checksums.yaml; Added installation output interpretation guide (green checkmarks, yellow warnings, red X meanings).","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r1z5","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-qxj8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:44:39.52501-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r2np","title":"[EPIC] Post-Install Transition Experience","description":"# Epic: Post-Install Transition Experience ## Problem Statement After installation, users must reconnect as ubuntu (instead of root). This transition has several pitfalls: 1. **New SSH command format**: The reconnect command uses `-i ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519` which is NEW. Users don't understand they're now using key auth instead of password. 2. **Key auth failure**: If key auth doesn't work, they're stuck. No clear fallback path. 3. **Powerlevel10k wizard surprise**: On first zsh login, p10k may launch a configuration wizard. This is unexpected and confusing. 4. **Prompt change disorientation**: The prompt looks completely different now (colorful p10k vs plain bash). Users might think something is wrong. ## Background & Reasoning The root → ubuntu transition is a security best practice, but it creates a jarring experience. Users went from password auth as root to key auth as ubuntu. The shell also changes from bash to zsh with p10k theming. Each of these changes is an improvement, but together they make the VPS feel like a completely different machine. Users need preparation for these changes. ## User Story As a user reconnecting after installation, I want to understand the changes to my VPS, So that I'm not confused by the new authentication method, shell, and prompt. ## Success Criteria 1. User understands the new SSH command uses their key 2. User knows they won't need a password anymore 3. User knows to press 'q' if p10k wizard appears 4. User understands the colorful prompt is normal and good 5. User has a fallback if key auth fails ## Scope - Enhance reconnect-ubuntu page with key vs password explanation - Add p10k wizard handling instructions - Add "Your prompt looks different now" explanation - Add key auth troubleshooting - Add fallback instructions if key doesn't work ## Dependencies - Epic F: Installation (user must have completed installation) ## UI/UX Requirements - Maintain current polish for desktop and mobile - Key auth explanation should be clear but not overwhelming - P10k handling should be a GuideTip - Prompt change should include before/after visual ## Technical Approach 1. Add explanation of key auth vs password above SSH command 2. Add GuideTip about p10k wizard (press q to skip) 3. Add OutputPreview showing new prompt style 4. Add troubleshooting section for key auth failures 5. Consider adding visual comparison: old prompt vs new ## Estimated Effort Small-Medium (1 wizard page enhancement) ## Priority Justification P1 (High) - Reconnection failure after successful install is very frustrating.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:36:12.00915-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:55:04.567741-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:55:04.567741-05:00","close_reason":"All 5 success criteria verified: (1) key auth explanation in reconnect-ubuntu:131-141, (2) 'No password needed' AlertCard:143-146, (3) p10k wizard handling:170-189, (4) colorful prompt explained:165,203,235, (5) key auth fallback/troubleshooting in verify-key-connection:120-137","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r2np","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-vydu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:38:04.85656-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r3gd","title":"Align agent aliases across zshrc and docs","description":"Found mismatch: zsh aliases for cod/gmi didn't match AGENTS/README/onboarding. Update acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc, onboarding lesson 04, and agent command examples to use codex --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox and gemini --yolo; align README snippet.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:15:58.950788-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T19:30:11.495408-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T19:30:11.495408-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 45ea80d - namespaced SCRIPT_DIR and aligned agent aliases"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r50f","title":"State library: fail clearly on jq update errors","description":"scripts/lib/state.sh has several jq command substitutions that don’t check exit status (e.g., state_step_update/state_phase_complete/state_phase_fail/state_save/state_migrate_v1_to_v2/state_upgrade_set_error). In non-strict callers this can silently propagate bad state updates. Add explicit error handling so failures return nonzero without writing invalid/empty state.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:54:49.303573-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T22:59:19.847092-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T22:59:19.847092-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: added explicit jq failure handling in state.sh write/update helpers and initialized selection arrays early in install.sh; shellcheck + bash -n passed."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r56u","title":"TASK: Integrate acfs info into CLI and zshrc","description":"# TASK: Integrate acfs info into CLI and zshrc ## Context Wire up the info command into the existing acfs CLI function. ## Implementation ### Update acfs() function in acfs.zshrc ```bash acfs() { case "$1" in info) shift source "${ACFS_HOME:-$HOME/.acfs}/scripts/lib/info.sh" acfs_info "$@" ;; # ... existing cases esac } ``` ### Update acfs help output Add info to the help text: ``` acfs info Show environment quick reference acfs info --json Output as JSON acfs info --html Output as HTML ``` ### Create acfs_info dispatcher ```bash acfs_info() { case "$1" in --json) info_render_json ;; --html) info_render_html ;; --minimal) info_render_minimal ;; --help) info_show_help ;; *) info_render_terminal ;; esac } ``` ## Testing ```bash acfs info # Terminal output acfs info --json # JSON output acfs info --json | jq # Validate JSON acfs info --html > /tmp/test.html # HTML output acfs help # Shows info in list ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] acfs info works - [ ] acfs info --json works - [ ] acfs info --html works - [ ] acfs help shows info command - [ ] Help text is accurate","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:57:30.415862-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T15:07:45.631863-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T15:07:45.631863-05:00","close_reason":"Complete implementation: info.sh with terminal, JSON, HTML, minimal output formats; Pass command + args as separate argv entries (e.g., bash --dir /Users/jemanuel/mcp_agent_mail --yes).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:27:50.18235-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T22:31:32.626103-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T22:31:32.626103-05:00","close_reason":"Completed"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r6xz","title":"Feature: SSH Key Guidance Enhancement","description":"","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:44:14.120191-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T13:10:42.709063-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T13:10:42.709063-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented SSH Key Guidance Enhancement: added ~/.ssh folder explanation, enhanced file location guidance, and improved passphrase explanation in generate-ssh-key wizard page"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-r7xo","title":"Default-collapse non-essential service tiers on accounts page","description":"# Task: Improve Accounts Page Information Hierarchy ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Authentication Flow for Headless VPS ## Problem The accounts page shows 8+ services at once, which is overwhelming. Even with tiers, seeing everything expanded makes users feel like they need ALL the services before proceeding. ## Current State - Essential tier: expanded (correct) - Recommended tier: expanded (should be collapsed) - Optional tier: expanded (should be collapsed) ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/accounts/page.tsx Change TIER_META defaults: \`\`\`tsx const TIER_META: Record = { essential: { // ... defaultOpen: true, // Keep open - these are required }, recommended: { // ... defaultOpen: false, // CHANGE: Collapse by default }, optional: { // ... defaultOpen: false, // Keep collapsed }, }; \`\`\` Also add clearer messaging: \`\`\`tsx Focus on Essential first. You only need GitHub and Claude Code to get started. The other services can wait until you need them — the tools will prompt you when it's time. \`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Recommended tier collapsed by default - [ ] Optional tier collapsed by default - [ ] Clear messaging that only Essential is needed now - [ ] Tiers still expandable for curious users - [ ] Progress bar only tracks Essential tier ## Why This Matters Overwhelming users with choices leads to decision paralysis and abandonment. A focused experience (2 services vs 8) is more achievable.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:55:36.278776-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:02:53.516624-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:02:53.516624-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented: accounts/page.tsx TierSection has defaultOpen:false for non-essential tiers"} -{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-rbrf","title":"Task: Add Linux skip option to OS selection","description":"","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:46:14.287345-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T13:15:16.049459-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-rbrf","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-qxj8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:52:43.784088-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-rbrf","title":"Task: Add Linux skip option to OS selection","description":"","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:46:14.287345-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T13:15:36.853331-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-rbrf","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-qxj8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T23:52:43.784088-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-rbu","title":"Implement auth status check functions for each service","description":"## Task Create functions to check authentication status for each service. ## Location `packages/onboard/src/lib/authChecks.ts` ## Implementation ```typescript import { execSync } from 'child_process'; import * as fs from 'fs'; import * as path from 'path'; import * as os from 'os'; export interface AuthStatus { authenticated: boolean; details?: string; // e.g., username, IP, etc. } /** * Check if Tailscale is authenticated and connected */ export function checkTailscale(): AuthStatus { try { const result = execSync('tailscale status --json', { encoding: 'utf-8' }); const status = JSON.parse(result); if (status.BackendState === 'Running') { const ip = execSync('tailscale ip -4', { encoding: 'utf-8' }).trim(); return { authenticated: true, details: `IP: ${ip}` }; } return { authenticated: false }; } catch { return { authenticated: false }; } } /** * Check if Claude Code is authenticated */ export function checkClaude(): AuthStatus { const configPaths = [ path.join(os.homedir(), '.claude', 'config.json'), path.join(os.homedir(), '.config', 'claude', 'config.json'), ]; for (const configPath of configPaths) { if (fs.existsSync(configPath)) { try { const config = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(configPath, 'utf-8')); if (config.user?.email) { return { authenticated: true, details: config.user.email }; } return { authenticated: true }; } catch { // File exists but unreadable, assume authenticated return { authenticated: true }; } } } return { authenticated: false }; } /** * Check if Codex CLI is authenticated */ export function checkCodex(): AuthStatus { const authPath = path.join(os.homedir(), '.codex', 'auth.json'); if (fs.existsSync(authPath)) { try { const auth = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(authPath, 'utf-8')); if (auth.access_token) { return { authenticated: true }; } } catch { // File corrupted } } return { authenticated: false }; } /** * Check if Gemini CLI is authenticated */ export function checkGemini(): AuthStatus { const credPaths = [ path.join(os.homedir(), '.config', 'gemini', 'credentials.json'), ]; // Also check for GOOGLE_API_KEY env var if (process.env.GOOGLE_API_KEY) { return { authenticated: true, details: 'via GOOGLE_API_KEY' }; } for (const credPath of credPaths) { if (fs.existsSync(credPath)) { return { authenticated: true }; } } return { authenticated: false }; } /** * Check if GitHub is configured */ export function checkGitHub(): AuthStatus { try { const result = execSync('gh auth status', { encoding: 'utf-8', stdio: 'pipe' }); if (result.includes('Logged in to')) { const match = result.match(/Logged in to .+ as (\S+)/); return { authenticated: true, details: match?.[1] }; } } catch { // Not authenticated } return { authenticated: false }; } /** * Check if Vercel CLI is authenticated */ export function checkVercel(): AuthStatus { try { const result = execSync('vercel whoami', { encoding: 'utf-8', stdio: 'pipe' }); if (result.trim()) { return { authenticated: true, details: result.trim() }; } } catch { // Not authenticated } return { authenticated: false }; } /** * Check if Supabase CLI is authenticated */ export function checkSupabase(): AuthStatus { const configPath = path.join(os.homedir(), '.supabase', 'config.toml'); if (fs.existsSync(configPath)) { return { authenticated: true }; } return { authenticated: false }; } /** * Check if Cloudflare/Wrangler is authenticated */ export function checkWrangler(): AuthStatus { try { const result = execSync('wrangler whoami', { encoding: 'utf-8', stdio: 'pipe' }); if (!result.includes('not authenticated')) { const match = result.match(/email: (.+)/i); return { authenticated: true, details: match?.[1] }; } } catch { // Not authenticated } return { authenticated: false }; } // Master check function export const AUTH_CHECKS: Record AuthStatus> = { tailscale: checkTailscale, 'claude-code': checkClaude, 'codex-cli': checkCodex, 'gemini-cli': checkGemini, github: checkGitHub, vercel: checkVercel, supabase: checkSupabase, cloudflare: checkWrangler, }; export function checkAllServices(): Record { const results: Record = {}; for (const [id, check] of Object.entries(AUTH_CHECKS)) { results[id] = check(); } return results; } ``` ## Testing Create unit tests: ```typescript // packages/onboard/src/lib/__tests__/authChecks.test.ts describe('authChecks', () => { it('checkTailscale returns correct status', () => { // Mock execSync // Test both authenticated and not authenticated cases }); // etc. }); ``` ## Considerations 1. **Error handling**: All checks should fail gracefully 2. **Performance**: Checks should be fast (no network calls) 3. **Cross-platform**: Handle macOS/Linux differences (onboard may run locally) 4. **Binary availability**: Don't error if binary not installed","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T17:02:18.880204-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:46:23.998833-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:46:23.998833-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented authChecks.ts and bun tests","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-rbu","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-8h4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T17:03:25.527838-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-rbu","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-14w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T17:03:26.053877-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-re2","title":"Design AccountsStep UI component","description":"## Task Design and implement the AccountsStep wizard component. ## Location `apps/web/components/wizard/AccountsStep.tsx` ## Component Structure ```tsx // apps/web/components/wizard/AccountsStep.tsx import { useState, useEffect } from 'react'; import { SERVICES, Service, ServicePriority } from '@/lib/services'; import { ServiceCard } from './ServiceCard'; import { GoogleSsoCallout } from './GoogleSsoCallout'; import { useLocalStorage } from '@/hooks/useLocalStorage'; interface AccountsStepProps { onNext: () => void; onBack: () => void; } export function AccountsStep({ onNext, onBack }: AccountsStepProps) { const [checkedServices, setCheckedServices] = useLocalStorage( 'acfs-accounts-checked', [] ); const toggleService = (id: string) => { setCheckedServices(prev => prev.includes(id) ? prev.filter(s => s !== id) : [...prev, id] ); }; const priorityGroups: { priority: ServicePriority; title: string; description: string }[] = [ { priority: 'strongly-recommended', title: 'Strongly Recommended', description: 'Set these up first for the best experience', }, { priority: 'recommended', title: 'Recommended', description: 'High-value services for most users', }, { priority: 'optional', title: 'Optional', description: 'Nice to have, set up when needed', }, ]; const checkedCount = checkedServices.length; const totalCount = SERVICES.length; return (
{/* Header */}

Set Up Your Accounts

These services will supercharge your vibe coding experience. Create accounts now while you wait for the installer to run.

{/* Google SSO Callout */} {/* Progress */}
{checkedCount} of {totalCount} ready
{/* Service Groups */} {priorityGroups.map(group => { const services = SERVICES.filter(s => s.priority === group.priority); if (services.length === 0) return null; return (

{group.title}

{group.description}

{services.map(service => ( toggleService(service.id)} /> ))}
); })} {/* Navigation */}
); } ``` ## Sub-Components ### ServiceCard ```tsx // apps/web/components/wizard/ServiceCard.tsx interface ServiceCardProps { service: Service; checked: boolean; onToggle: () => void; } export function ServiceCard({ service, checked, onToggle }: ServiceCardProps) { return (
{/* Checkbox */} {/* Logo */} {/* Content */}

{service.name}

{service.supportsGoogleSso && ( Google SSO )}

{service.shortDescription}

{/* Expanded content (always visible or on hover) */}

{service.whyNeeded}

{service.supportsGoogleSso && service.googleSsoUrl ? ( Sign up with Google ) : ( Sign up )} {service.alternativeAuth && ( )}
); } ``` ### GoogleSsoCallout ```tsx // apps/web/components/wizard/GoogleSsoCallout.tsx export function GoogleSsoCallout() { return (
💡

Pro Tip: Use Your Google Account

Most services support Google Sign-In. Using the same Google account for everything means fewer passwords and better security.

Make sure your Google account has{' '} 2-Step Verification enabled .

); } ``` ## Design Considerations 1. **Mobile-first**: Cards stack on mobile, 2-column on desktop 2. **Accessibility**: All interactive elements keyboard accessible 3. **Visual feedback**: Clear checked/unchecked states 4. **Non-blocking**: User can skip without creating all accounts 5. **Persistent state**: localStorage survives page reload 6. **External links**: Open in new tab with proper rel attributes ## Integration - Add to wizard step sequence in `apps/web/app/wizard/page.tsx` - Position after VPS setup, before "Run Installer" step","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T17:00:28.150149-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:30:23.960544-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:30:23.960544-05:00","close_reason":"Completed via accounts/page.tsx which includes: ServiceCard components, Google SSO callout, progress indicator, checkbox state, category grouping, skip/continue navigation. Page approach used instead of separate component file.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-re2","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-14w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T17:03:24.723387-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-rhvw","title":"TASK: Implement acfs info --json output","description":"# TASK: Implement acfs info --json output ## Context Add JSON output format for scripting and integration with other tools. ## Implementation ### Function ```bash info_render_json() { cat < "12+ services presented simultaneously... User thought: 'Do I really need ALL > of these? This is going to take hours!'" For beginners, seeing GitHub, Anthropic, OpenAI, Google AI, Cloudflare, Vercel, Supabase, and more creates: 1. **Decision Fatigue**: Which ones are truly required? 2. **Time Anxiety**: This looks like hours of work 3. **Abandonment Risk**: Users may give up or skip important ones ## Proposed Solution: Two-Tier Approach ### Tier 1: Essential (Show First, Required) Only 2-3 services that are TRULY needed to start: 1. **GitHub** - Required for code backup (the only backup!) 2. **Anthropic Claude** - Primary AI agent for the workflow That's it. Everything else can wait. ### Tier 2: Recommended (Collapsed, Do Later) Show these in a collapsed section: - **OpenAI** (for Codex CLI) - **Google AI** (for Gemini CLI) ### Tier 3: Optional (Even More Collapsed, When Needed) - Vercel (for deployment) - Cloudflare (for domains) - Supabase (for databases) - Docker Hub - etc. ## UI Design \`\`\` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Set Up Accounts │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ │ │ ⚡ ESSENTIAL (Do These Now) │ │ ───────────────────────────── │ │ These two accounts are required to start: │ │ │ │ [GitHub Card - expanded] │ │ [Anthropic Claude Card - expanded] │ │ │ │ ▶ RECOMMENDED (After First Project) ────────────── │ │ Add these when you want more AI agents │ │ │ │ ▶ OPTIONAL (When You Need Them) ───────────────── │ │ For deployment, databases, and more │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ \`\`\` ## Post-Install Authentication After each account is created, show the authentication command: ### For GitHub: 1. Sign up at github.com 2. On your VPS, run: \`gh auth login\` 3. Follow the prompts (browser flow) ### For Claude: 1. Subscribe to Claude Max at anthropic.com 2. On your VPS, run: \`claude\` 3. Complete browser login ## Verification UI Add verification step for each service: \`\`\` [✓] GitHub - authenticated [ ] Claude - needs authentication \`\`\` Could even check this programmatically: - GitHub: \`gh auth status\` returns 0 - Claude: check for ~/.claude/credentials or similar ## Data Model Updates Modify lib/services.ts: \`\`\`typescript interface Service { // ... existing fields tier: 'essential' | 'recommended' | 'optional'; sortOrder: number; // within tier } \`\`\` Then update SERVICES array with tier assignments. ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Essential tier shows only GitHub and Claude - [ ] Recommended tier is collapsed by default - [ ] Optional tier is collapsed by default - [ ] Clear explanation of when to do each tier - [ ] Post-install auth commands shown for each service - [ ] Page loads faster (less to render initially) - [ ] Mobile experience improved (less scrolling) ## Dependencies - None - can be done in parallel with other features ## Migration Notes - lib/services.ts needs tier field added - Page component needs section logic - Existing service data preserved, just reorganized ## Success Metrics - Reduced time spent on accounts page - Higher completion rate for essential services - User feedback on cognitive load ## Considerations 1. **What about existing users?** They can still expand all sections 2. **What if user needs Vercel first?** They can expand Optional section 3. **International users?** Some may need different services first 4. **Enterprise users?** May need all services - that's fine, just expand","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:17:13.186334-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T19:39:53.635076-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T19:39:53.635076-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-rn2q","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-umqa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:17:50.548497-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-rs8j","title":"web: add commands+glossary links to Learning Hub","description":"Update `apps/web/app/learn/page.tsx` Quick Reference section to include links to the new `/learn/commands` and `/learn/glossary` pages (alongside existing Agent Commands / NTM). No new files.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:04:39.076357-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T19:05:53.643481-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T19:05:53.643481-05:00","close_reason":"Updated /learn Quick Reference cards to include /learn/commands and /learn/glossary."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-s25","title":"Create auth lesson flow in onboard TUI","description":"## Task Implement the interactive authentication lesson in the onboard TUI. ## Location `packages/onboard/src/lessons/auth/` ## Lesson Structure ### Main Entry (index.ts) ```typescript // packages/onboard/src/lessons/auth/index.ts import { Lesson, LessonStep } from '../../types'; import { checkAllServices, AUTH_CHECKS } from '../../lib/authChecks'; import { tailscaleLesson } from './tailscale'; import { claudeLesson } from './claude'; import { codexLesson } from './codex'; import { geminiLesson } from './gemini'; const serviceLessons = [ tailscaleLesson, claudeLesson, codexLesson, geminiLesson, ]; export const authLesson: Lesson = { id: 'auth', title: 'Authenticate Services', description: 'Connect your coding tools and services', async run(ui) { // Check current auth status const statuses = checkAllServices(); // Show overview ui.clear(); ui.header('Service Authentication'); ui.text('Let\'s connect your services. We\'ll go through each one.'); ui.blank(); for (const lesson of serviceLessons) { const status = statuses[lesson.serviceId]; const icon = status.authenticated ? '✓' : '○'; const detail = status.authenticated ? status.details || 'Authenticated' : 'Not configured'; ui.text(` ${icon} ${lesson.name.padEnd(15)} - ${detail}`); } ui.blank(); await ui.prompt('Press Enter to start...'); // Run each lesson for (const lesson of serviceLessons) { const status = statuses[lesson.serviceId]; if (status.authenticated) { // Already authenticated, ask if want to re-auth ui.clear(); ui.header(lesson.name); ui.success(`Already authenticated${status.details ? `: ${status.details}` : ''}!`); const choice = await ui.prompt('[Enter to continue, r to re-authenticate]'); if (choice.toLowerCase() !== 'r') { continue; } } await lesson.run(ui); // Re-check status after lesson const newStatus = AUTH_CHECKS[lesson.serviceId](); statuses[lesson.serviceId] = newStatus; } // Summary ui.clear(); ui.header('Authentication Complete!'); ui.blank(); const authenticated = Object.values(statuses).filter(s => s.authenticated).length; const total = Object.keys(statuses).length; ui.text(`${authenticated} of ${total} services authenticated.`); ui.blank(); for (const lesson of serviceLessons) { const status = statuses[lesson.serviceId]; const icon = status.authenticated ? '✓' : '✗'; ui.text(` ${icon} ${lesson.name}`); } ui.blank(); await ui.prompt('Press Enter to return to menu...'); }, }; ``` ### Individual Service Lessons ```typescript // packages/onboard/src/lessons/auth/tailscale.ts import { ServiceLesson } from '../../types'; import { checkTailscale } from '../../lib/authChecks'; export const tailscaleLesson: ServiceLesson = { serviceId: 'tailscale', name: 'Tailscale', async run(ui) { ui.clear(); ui.header('Tailscale Setup'); ui.blank(); ui.text('Tailscale gives you secure remote access to your VPS.'); ui.text('Once connected, you can SSH from anywhere!'); ui.blank(); ui.text('Run this command:'); ui.blank(); ui.code('sudo tailscale up'); ui.blank(); ui.text('This will open a browser (or show a URL).'); ui.text('Log in with your Google account.'); ui.blank(); const choice = await ui.prompt('[Press Enter when done, or s to skip]'); if (choice.toLowerCase() === 's') { ui.warn('Skipped. You can set up Tailscale later.'); await ui.sleep(1500); return; } // Verify authentication ui.text('Checking Tailscale status...'); await ui.sleep(1000); const status = checkTailscale(); if (status.authenticated) { ui.blank(); ui.success('Tailscale connected!'); ui.text(`Your Tailscale IP: ${status.details}`); ui.blank(); ui.text('Now you can SSH to this machine from any device'); ui.text('with Tailscale installed.'); ui.blank(); await ui.prompt('[Press Enter to continue]'); } else { ui.blank(); ui.warn('Tailscale not yet connected.'); ui.text('You can try again or set it up later.'); const retry = await ui.prompt('[r to retry, Enter to continue]'); if (retry.toLowerCase() === 'r') { return this.run(ui); // Recursive retry } } }, }; ``` ## UI Framework Use a simple TUI framework. Options: - `blessed` - Classic Node.js TUI - `ink` - React for CLIs - `prompts` - Simple and lightweight Recommend `prompts` + custom rendering for simplicity: ```typescript // packages/onboard/src/ui.ts import prompts from 'prompts'; import chalk from 'chalk'; export class UI { clear() { console.clear(); } header(text: string) { console.log(chalk.bold.blue(`\n${'═'.repeat(60)}`)); console.log(chalk.bold.blue(` ${text}`)); console.log(chalk.bold.blue(`${'═'.repeat(60)}\n`)); } text(text: string) { console.log(text); } blank() { console.log(); } code(text: string) { console.log(chalk.bgGray.white(` ${text} `)); } success(text: string) { console.log(chalk.green(`✓ ${text}`)); } warn(text: string) { console.log(chalk.yellow(`⚠ ${text}`)); } async prompt(message: string): Promise { const response = await prompts({ type: 'text', name: 'value', message, }); return response.value || ''; } async sleep(ms: number) { return new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, ms)); } } ``` ## Integration Add to main menu in `packages/onboard/src/index.ts`: ```typescript import { authLesson } from './lessons/auth'; const lessons = [ linuxBasicsLesson, terminalLesson, gitLesson, authLesson, // NEW agentLesson, ]; ```","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T17:02:59.962782-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:45:31.696507-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:45:31.696507-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented in bash: onboard.sh has show_auth_flow() (lines 318-436) with full TUI menu, show_auth_service() for individual services, gum integration with fallback. Menu option [a] for 'Authenticate Services' added. Progress tracking and all 8 services supported.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-s25","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-rbu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T17:03:25.780702-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-s25","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-bt5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T17:03:26.298833-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} @@ -335,7 +335,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-vydu","title":"[EPIC] Installation Resilience & Progress Feedback","description":"# Epic: Installation Resilience & Progress Feedback ## Problem Statement The 10-15 minute installation is anxiety-inducing for beginners: 1. **SSH disconnection panic**: If their WiFi hiccups, they lose connection and think they broke the VPS. They don't know the installer keeps running. 2. **Wall of text confusion**: Lots of output scrolling by. They don't know if it's working, failing, or what any of it means. 3. **curl | bash skepticism**: Security-aware users worry about piping curl to bash. The "View source" link exists but isn't prominent enough. 4. **Command gibberish**: `-fsSL` means nothing to them. ## Background & Reasoning Long-running installations are inherently risky for beginners. Any unexpected behavior triggers panic. The installer IS idempotent and resilient, but users don't know that. We need to: 1. Set expectations BEFORE they run the command 2. Explain what they'll see DURING installation 3. Provide clear recovery instructions IF something goes wrong 4. Address security concerns proactively ## User Story As a beginner running the installer, I want to understand what's happening and what to do if something goes wrong, So that I can wait patiently and recover from any issues. ## Success Criteria 1. User knows that disconnection is recoverable (just SSH back in) 2. User understands progress indicators (green checkmarks = good) 3. User knows to wait for "Installation complete" message 4. User can find and understand the install script source 5. User understands the curl command parts at a high level ## Scope - Add disconnection recovery instructions prominently - Add "What you'll see" section explaining output format - Make "View source" more prominent for security-conscious users - Add optional command breakdown for curious users - Consider adding a "If something fails" troubleshooting section ## Dependencies - Epic D: First SSH Connection (user must be connected) ## UI/UX Requirements - Maintain current polish for desktop and mobile - Disconnection warning should be AlertCard variant="warning" - Output explanation should use OutputPreview component - Source link should be visually distinct (security/trust badge) ## Technical Approach 1. Add AlertCard about disconnection recovery near top 2. Add OutputPreview showing example progress output 3. Add expandable "What is this command doing?" with breakdown 4. Enhance "View source" with trust badge styling 5. Add GuideCaution for "If installation seems stuck" ## Estimated Effort Small-Medium (1 wizard page enhancement) ## Priority Justification P1 (High) - Installation failure/abandonment means complete user loss.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:35:47.401107-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:57:18.94433-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:57:18.94433-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented command breakdown section explaining curl flags, | bash piping, and --yes/--mode options. Also addresses curl | bash security concerns. Page already had disconnection recovery, progress indicators, and view source links. All success criteria met.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-vydu","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-q7r3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:37:59.599308-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-w0q","title":"install.sh: dry-run prints misleading completion banner","description":"In --dry-run mode, install.sh skips all phases but still prints the full 'ACFS Installation Complete' summary. {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-wfi2","title":"Add installation progress feedback explanation","description":"# Task: Add Installation Progress Feedback Explanation ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Installation Resilience & Progress Feedback ## Problem During the 10-15 minute installation, users see a "wall of text" scrolling by. They don't know: - Is it working or failing? - What do the colors mean? - How far along are we? - When should I worry? ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/run-installer/page.tsx Add "What You'll See" section: \`\`\`tsx

The installer shows progress as it works. Here's how to read it:

✓

Green checkmarks = Success

That step completed successfully.

!

Yellow warnings = Minor issue

Something optional was skipped. Usually fine.

✗

Red X = Step failed

The installer will retry or work around it.

Lots of scrolling text is normal! Just watch for the final "Installation complete" message. If you see mostly green checkmarks, you're good. \`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Explains color coding (green/yellow/red) - [ ] Shows visual examples of each state - [ ] Reassures that scrolling text is normal - [ ] Tells user what to wait for (final message) ## Why This Matters Anxiety during installation leads to premature abandonment or unnecessary panic. Users who understand the progress indicators can wait patiently.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:54:50.363193-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:02:35.446502-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:02:35.446502-05:00","close_reason":"Already covered: run-installer/page.tsx explains progress messages and idempotent behavior"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-whw3","title":"Update README OS requirement to Ubuntu 25.10","description":"README still references Ubuntu 24.04+ in badges and diagrams. Update to reflect current requirement: target Ubuntu 25.10 with auto-upgrade from 22.04+ (per AGENTS.md).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:52:58.302284-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T18:54:59.003219-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T18:54:59.003219-05:00","close_reason":"Updated README Ubuntu requirement to 25.10"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-wn0","title":"errors.sh: prioritize specific error pattern matches","description":"When multiple ERROR_PATTERNS substrings overlap (e.g., 'timed out' vs 'curl: (28) Connection timed out'), the current iteration order is nondeterministic. Sort patterns by length so we select the most specific match and provide the best suggested fix.","acceptance_criteria":"- get_error_pattern prefers longer/more specific patterns\n- get_suggested_fix and is_known_error use that matching","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:00:24.84378-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T18:00:43.226729-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T18:00:43.226729-05:00","close_reason":"Sort ERROR_PATTERNS by length so error matching prefers specific patterns; refactor callers to use get_error_pattern."}
